problem: Healthcare systems are fragmented, paper-heavy, and prioritize billing over patient outcomes.
solution: A unified digital health platform that connects patients, providers, and payers in one experience.

insights.title: Interoperability Mandate
insights.description: CMS interoperability rules are driving $5B+ in healthcare IT spending. Your timing aligns with regulatory tailwinds.

insights.title: Clinician Burnout
insights.description: 86% of clinicians report burnout from administrative tools. A UX-first approach is a massive differentiator.

insights.title: Sales Cycle Reality
insights.description: Hospital procurement averages 12-18 months. Plan your runway accordingly and start with outpatient clinics.

insights.title: Integration First
insights.description: Build Epic and Cerner integrations before adding features. Without EMR integration, your product won't get evaluated.

website.summary: Clean, professional website with strong clinical imagery, but lacks technical depth on integrations.
recommendations: Lead with interoperability — list supported EMRs prominently
Add a 'Security & Compliance' section with HIPAA attestation
Include case studies from pilot hospitals or clinics
Offer a self-service sandbox for IT teams to test integrations

brand.mission: To make healthcare technology work for patients, clinicians, and administrators alike.

logos.description: Leaf combined with a heartbeat line — natural meets clinical
Simplified cross symbol with overlapping gradient layers
Abstract DNA helix forming the first letter of your brand

icp.title: Chief Medical Information Officer
icp.role: Clinical technology decision-maker at a healthcare organization
icp.description: A healthcare technology executive responsible for digital transformation, drowning in legacy EMR systems, interoperability nightmares, and the pressure to improve patient outcomes while reducing costs.
painPoints: EMR systems don't talk to each other — patient data is siloed
HIPAA compliance makes every engineering change a legal review
Clinician burnout from poorly designed digital tools is at crisis levels
Telehealth infrastructure is stuck at 2019 quality
goals: Achieve true interoperability between EMR systems
Improve patient outcomes with data-driven clinical insights
Meet evolving regulatory and reporting requirements
objections: "HIPAA compliance is too risky with third parties"
"How do you integrate with our existing stack?"
recommendations: Build an EMR integration directory showing compatibility with Epic, Cerner, and Meditech
Publish a HIPAA compliance whitepaper before the first sales call
Create a clinician ROI calculator showing hours saved per week

competitors.name: Epic Systems
competitors.strength: Dominant EMR provider, deep hospital integration
competitors.weakness: Terrible UX, expensive, requires IT teams to operate

competitors.name: DrChrono
competitors.weakness: Limited to ambulatory care, no hospital features

revenue.justification: At ideation stage, revenue projections are speculative. Focus on customer discovery interviews to validate willingness to pay before building pricing.
revenue.analysis: Mental Health AI — At ideation stage, revenue projections are speculative. Focus on customer discovery interviews to validate willingness to pay before building pricing.

items.title: Patient intake digitalization MVP
items.description: Replace paper forms with tablet-based intake

items.title: Provider dashboard prototype
items.description: Single-pane view of patient schedule and history

items.title: Iterate based on usage data
items.description: Ship 3 feature iterations based on user feedback, measure NPS > 40

items.title: Achieve first revenue
items.description: Convert 10% of free users to paid, reach $2K MRR

items.title: Scale acquisition channels
items.description: Identify 2 channels with CAC < LTV/3, spend $5K/mo profitably

items.title: Hire first employee
items.description: Bring on a full-stack engineer or sales lead to accelerate growth

verdict.summary: Mental Health AI: Your startup scored 47/100. founderFit is your strongest dimension (70/100). distribution needs the most attention (20/100).

market.label: Large & Fragmented
market.description: Healthcare IT is a $500B+ market. Fragmented buyer landscape creates both opportunity and complexity.

timing.label: Cautious Timing
timing.description: Post-COVID digital health adoption has slowed. Sales cycles remain 12-18 months.

competition.label: Entrenched Incumbents
competition.description: Epic and Cerner control 70%+ of hospital EMR market. Integration-dependent market.

defensibility.label: Compliance Moat
defensibility.description: HIPAA, SOC 2, and FDA clearance create significant barriers. Integration depth adds switching costs.

founderFit.label: Early Stage
founderFit.description: At ideation, founder-market fit is assumed based on your problem choice. Validate through customer discovery.

distribution.label: Complex Healthcare Sales
distribution.description: Hospital procurement requires multiple stakeholders. Starting with clinics bypasses enterprise sales.

revenue.label: Per-Seat, Long-Term
revenue.description: Hospital software contracts are high-value (6-figure ACV) but long sales cycles.

strengths.explanation: Strong founder-market fit. You deeply understand the problem.
Your market has potential but faces growth or competitive challenges.

weaknesses.explanation: Customer acquisition is a critical risk. Rethink your distribution strategy.
You face entrenched competitors with significant advantages.

fatalRisks: Critical weakness in distribution: scored 20/100. Customer acquisition is a critical risk. Rethink your distribution strategy.
verdict.suggestedPivot: Your weakest dimension is distribution (20/100). Consider rethinking your Healthtech strategy to address this gap. Launch a self-serve onboarding flow

improvementPaths.action: Work in the industry you're disrupting for 3 months
improvementPaths.risk: Hire a CEO who doesn't understand the problem

improvementPaths.action: Narrow from broad market to a specific vertical
improvementPaths.risk: Expand too early into adjacent verticals

improvementPaths.action: Ship MVP and get first 10 design partners
improvementPaths.risk: Delay launch by 6+ months for perfection

improvementPaths.action: File provisional patents or build proprietary data assets
Identify and amplify your unfair advantage
improvementPaths.risk: A well-funded competitor enters your space
Rely entirely on outbound enterprise sales

roast.verdict: Mental Health AI — Healthcare is the hardest market to sell into. The buyer isn't the user, the sales cycle is 12-18 months, and one compliance misstep ends the company.
risks: HIPAA compliance is expensive ($50K-$200K for initial audit)
Hospital procurement cycles kill early-stage startups
Clinical validation requires studies that take years
No revenue means every assumption about pricing is unvalidated
Ideation-stage startups have a 90% failure rate before reaching PMF
recommendations: Start with a B2B2C model: sell to clinics, not hospitals
Build for a single specialty (e.g., dermatology) before generalizing
Publish a security whitepaper on day one to shorten trust-building

items.feedback: HIPAA BAAs alone take 3-6 months to negotiate with hospital legal teams. Start the paperwork before you have customers.
Without Epic integration, you're not sellable to hospitals. Build that first, even if it's a manual export feature.
Starting with hospitals is a mistake. Target independent clinics and urgent care centers first — faster sales cycles.
You need clinical evidence. Publish a pilot study with measurable outcomes before approaching enterprise buyers.
Clinicians have 15-minute appointment slots. If your tool adds even one extra click, adoption will fail.
Hospitals hate per-seat pricing. Consider per-encounter or value-based pricing that aligns with outcomes.
